87: // UnmarshalJSON implements a custom unmarshaler that handles the historical rename
88: // from the "plan_file" JSON key to "task_file", mirroring session.InstanceData.UnmarshalJSON.
89: func (r *instanceRecord) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
90: 	type Alias instanceRecord
91: 	aux := &struct {
92: 		*Alias
93: 		PlanFile string `json:"plan_file,omitempty"`
94: 	}{
95: 		Alias: (*Alias)(r),
96: 	}
97: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, aux); err != nil {
98: 		return err
99: 	}
100: 	if r.TaskFile == "" && aux.PlanFile != "" {
101: 		r.TaskFile = aux.PlanFile
102: 	}
103: 	return nil
104: }

106: // statusLabel converts an instanceStatus to a lowercase text label.
107: func statusLabel(s instanceStatus) string {
108: 	switch s {
109: 	case instanceRunning:
110: 		return "running"
111: 	case instanceReady:
112: 		return "ready"
113: 	case instanceLoading:
114: 		return "loading"
115: 	case instancePaused:
116: 		return "paused"
117: 	default:
118: 		return fmt.Sprintf("unknown(%d)", s)
119: 	}
120: }

122: // whiteSpaceRe matches one or more whitespace characters for session name sanitisation.
123: var whiteSpaceRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
124: 
125: // kasTmuxName converts a human-readable instance title to the kas_-prefixed tmux
126: // session name used by the session package.  It replicates toKasTmuxName from
127: // session/tmux without importing that package (which would create a cycle:
128: // session/tmux → cmd → session/tmux).
129: func kasTmuxName(title string) string {
130: 	name := whiteSpaceRe.ReplaceAllString(title, "")
131: 	name = strings.ReplaceAll(name, ".", "_")
132: 	return "kas_" + name
133: }

135: // buildResumeCommand reconstructs the tmux program command string for a resumed
136: // instance.  It mirrors the env-var and flag injection performed by TmuxSession.Start()
137: // so that the resumed agent is indistinguishable from a freshly started one.
138: func buildResumeCommand(rec instanceRecord, worktreePath string) string {
139: 	program := rec.Program
140: 
141: 	// Append --dangerously-skip-permissions for Claude if originally enabled.
142: 	if rec.SkipPermissions && strings.HasSuffix(program, "claude") {
143: 		program += " --dangerously-skip-permissions"
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	// Append --agent flag for typed roles (planner, coder, reviewer, fixer).
147: 	if rec.AgentType != "" && !strings.Contains(program, "--agent") {
148: 		program += " --agent " + rec.AgentType
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// Append opencode log redirection so debug logs are preserved.
152: 	// Use rec.Program (the unmodified base) for the suffix check, matching
153: 	// TmuxSession.Start() which uses t.program — the local `program` variable
154: 	// may already have --agent appended, changing the suffix.
155: 	if strings.HasSuffix(rec.Program, "opencode") {
156: 		logDir := filepath.Join(worktreePath, ".kasmos", "logs")
157: 		if err := os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755); err == nil {
158: 			logFile := filepath.Join(logDir, kasTmuxName(rec.Title)+".log")
159: 			program += " --print-logs 2>>'" + logFile + "'"
160: 		}
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	// Prepend KASMOS_MANAGED=1 so the agent knows it is managed by kasmos.
164: 	program = "KASMOS_MANAGED=1 " + program
165: 
166: 	// Prepend task identity env vars for parallel wave execution.
167: 	if rec.TaskNumber > 0 {
168: 		program = fmt.Sprintf("KASMOS_TASK=%d KASMOS_WAVE=%d KASMOS_PEERS=%d %s",
169: 			rec.TaskNumber, rec.WaveNumber, rec.PeerCount, program)
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	return program
173: }

175: // executeInstanceList reads raw InstancesData from state, optionally filters by
176: // status, and formats the result as a text table or JSON array.
177: //
178: // statusFilters is optional; when provided only instances whose status label
179: // matches any of the given values are included.
180: func executeInstanceList(state config.StateManager, format string, statusFilters ...string) string {
181: 	raw := state.GetInstances()
182: 
183: 	var records []instanceRecord
184: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &records); err != nil {
185: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v\n", err)
186: 	}
187: 
188: 	// Apply optional status filter.
189: 	if len(statusFilters) > 0 {
190: 		filterSet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(statusFilters))
191: 		for _, f := range statusFilters {
192: 			filterSet[strings.ToLower(f)] = struct{}{}
193: 		}
194: 		filtered := records[:0]
195: 		for _, r := range records {
196: 			if _, ok := filterSet[statusLabel(r.Status)]; ok {
197: 				filtered = append(filtered, r)
198: 			}
199: 		}
200: 		records = filtered
201: 	}
202: 
203: 	if len(records) == 0 {
204: 		if format == "json" {
205: 			return "[]\n"
206: 		}
207: 		return "no instances\n"
208: 	}
209: 
210: 	switch format {
211: 	case "json":
212: 		type jsonRecord struct {
213: 			Title     string `json:"title"`
214: 			Status    string `json:"status"`
215: 			Branch    string `json:"branch"`
216: 			Program   string `json:"program"`
217: 			TaskFile  string `json:"task_file,omitempty"`
218: 			AgentType string `json:"agent_type,omitempty"`
219: 			CreatedAt string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
220: 		}
221: 		out := make([]jsonRecord, 0, len(records))
222: 		for _, r := range records {
223: 			var createdAt string
224: 			if !r.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
225: 				createdAt = r.CreatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
226: 			}
227: 			out = append(out, jsonRecord{
228: 				Title:     r.Title,
229: 				Status:    statusLabel(r.Status),
230: 				Branch:    r.Branch,
231: 				Program:   r.Program,
232: 				TaskFile:  r.TaskFile,
233: 				AgentType: r.AgentType,
234: 				CreatedAt: createdAt,
235: 			})
236: 		}
237: 		data, err := json.Marshal(out)
238: 		if err != nil {
239: 			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v\n", err)
240: 		}
241: 		return string(data) + "\n"
242: 
243: 	default: // "text"
244: 		var sb strings.Builder
245: 		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(&sb, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
246: 		fmt.Fprintln(w, "TITLE\tSTATUS\tBRANCH\tPROGRAM\tTASK")
247: 		for _, r := range records {
248: 			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n",
249: 				r.Title,
250: 				statusLabel(r.Status),
251: 				r.Branch,
252: 				r.Program,
253: 				r.TaskFile,
254: 			)
255: 		}
256: 		w.Flush()
257: 		return sb.String()
258: 	}
259: }
260: 
261: // loadInstanceRecords reads and parses the raw instance JSON from state.
262: func loadInstanceRecords(state config.StateManager) ([]instanceRecord, error) {
263: 	raw := state.GetInstances()
264: 	var records []instanceRecord
265: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &records); err != nil {
266: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse instances: %w", err)
267: 	}
268: 	return records, nil
269: }
270: 
271: // findInstanceData finds an instance record by title. It first tries an exact
272: // match, then falls back to a substring match. Returns an error when no match
273: // is found or when the substring matches more than one record (ambiguous).
274: func findInstanceData(records []instanceRecord, title string) (instanceRecord, error) {
275: 	// Exact match takes precedence.
276: 	for _, r := range records {
277: 		if r.Title == title {
278: 			return r, nil
279: 		}
280: 	}
281: 
282: 	// Substring fallback.
283: 	var matches []instanceRecord
284: 	for _, r := range records {
285: 		if strings.Contains(r.Title, title) {
286: 			matches = append(matches, r)
287: 		}
288: 	}
289: 	switch len(matches) {
290: 	case 0:
291: 		return instanceRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("instance not found: %q", title)
292: 	case 1:
293: 		return matches[0], nil
294: 	default:
295: 		names := make([]string, len(matches))
296: 		for i, m := range matches {
297: 			names[i] = m.Title
298: 		}
299: 		return instanceRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("ambiguous instance %q matches: %s", title, strings.Join(names, ", "))
300: 	}
301: }
302: 
303: // validateStatusForAction checks whether the instance is in a state compatible
304: // with the requested action and returns an error when it is not.
305: //
306: //   - kill:   allowed in any status
307: //   - pause:  not allowed when already paused
308: //   - resume: only allowed when paused
309: //   - send:   not allowed when paused
310: func validateStatusForAction(data instanceRecord, action string) error {
311: 	switch action {
312: 	case "kill":
313: 		// kill is allowed in any status
314: 		return nil
315: 	case "pause":
316: 		if data.Status == instancePaused {
317: 			return fmt.Errorf("instance is already paused")
318: 		}
319: 		return nil
320: 	case "resume":
321: 		if data.Status != instancePaused {
322: 			return fmt.Errorf("can only resume paused instances (current status: %s)", statusLabel(data.Status))
323: 		}
324: 		return nil
325: 	case "send":
326: 		if data.Status == instancePaused {
327: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot send prompt to a paused instance")
328: 		}
329: 		return nil
330: 	default:
331: 		return fmt.Errorf("unknown action: %q", action)
332: 	}
333: }
334: 
335: // removeInstanceFromState removes the named instance from persisted state.
336: // All remaining instance records are preserved verbatim — no fields are dropped.
337: func removeInstanceFromState(state config.StateManager, title string) error {
338: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
339: 	if err != nil {
340: 		return err
341: 	}
342: 	remaining := records[:0]
343: 	for _, r := range records {
344: 		if r.Title != title {
345: 			remaining = append(remaining, r)
346: 		}
347: 	}
348: 	raw, err := json.Marshal(remaining)
349: 	if err != nil {
350: 		return fmt.Errorf("marshal instances: %w", err)
351: 	}
352: 	return state.SaveInstances(raw)
353: }
354: 
355: // updateInstanceInState finds the named instance, applies updater to a copy,
356: // and persists the modified list back to state.
357: // All fields of every record (including unmodified ones) are preserved verbatim.
358: func updateInstanceInState(state config.StateManager, title string, updater func(*instanceRecord) error) error {
359: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
360: 	if err != nil {
361: 		return err
362: 	}
363: 	found := false
364: 	for i := range records {
365: 		if records[i].Title == title {
366: 			if err := updater(&records[i]); err != nil {
367: 				return err
368: 			}
369: 			found = true
370: 			break
371: 		}
372: 	}
373: 	if !found {
374: 		return fmt.Errorf("instance not found: %q", title)
375: 	}
376: 	raw, err := json.Marshal(records)
377: 	if err != nil {
378: 		return fmt.Errorf("marshal instances: %w", err)
379: 	}
380: 	return state.SaveInstances(raw)
381: }
382: 
383: // NewInstanceCmd builds the `kas instance` cobra command tree.
384: func NewInstanceCmd() *cobra.Command {
385: 	instanceCmd := &cobra.Command{
386: 		Use:   "instance",
387: 		Short: "manage agent instances (list, kill, pause, resume, send)",
388: 	}
389: 
390: 	// kas instance list
391: 	var format string
392: 	var statusFilter string
393: 	listCmd := &cobra.Command{
394: 		Use:   "list",
395: 		Short: "list all agent instances",
396: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
397: 			state := config.LoadState()
398: 			var filters []string
399: 			if statusFilter != "" {
400: 				filters = append(filters, statusFilter)
401: 			}
402: 			fmt.Print(executeInstanceList(state, format, filters...))
403: 			return nil
404: 		},
405: 	}
406: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&format, "format", "text", "output format: text or json")
407: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&statusFilter, "status", "", "filter by status (running, ready, loading, paused)")
408: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(listCmd)
409: 
410: 	// kas instance kill <title>
411: 	killCmd := &cobra.Command{
412: 		Use:   "kill <title>",
413: 		Short: "pause an agent instance and preserve its branch (safe kill)",
414: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
415: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
416: 			title := args[0]
417: 			state := config.LoadState()
418: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
419: 			if err != nil {
420: 				return err
421: 			}
422: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
423: 			if err != nil {
424: 				return err
425: 			}
426: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "kill"); err != nil {
427: 				return err
428: 			}
429: 			// Auto-commit dirty changes before removing worktree.
430: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" {
431: 				commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] auto-save from '%s' on %s (killed)",
432: 					rec.Title, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
433: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "add", "-A").Run()
434: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath,
435: 					"commit", "-m", commitMsg, "--allow-empty").Run()
436: 			}
437: 			// Kill tmux session (best-effort).
438: 			_ = exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title)).Run()
439: 			// Remove worktree but preserve branch.
440: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" && rec.Worktree.RepoPath != "" {
441: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath,
442: 					"worktree", "remove", "--force", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath).Run()
443: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath,
444: 					"worktree", "prune").Run()
445: 			}
446: 			// Set to paused instead of removing from state.
447: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
448: 				r.Status = instancePaused
449: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
450: 				return nil
451: 			}); err != nil {
452: 				return err
453: 			}
454: 			fmt.Printf("paused: %s (branch preserved)\n", rec.Title)
455: 			return nil
456: 		},
457: 	}
458: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(killCmd)
459: 
460: 	// kas instance pause <title>
461: 	pauseCmd := &cobra.Command{
462: 		Use:   "pause <title>",
463: 		Short: "pause an agent instance (saves branch, removes worktree)",
464: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
465: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
466: 			title := args[0]
467: 			state := config.LoadState()
468: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
469: 			if err != nil {
470: 				return err
471: 			}
472: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
473: 			if err != nil {
474: 				return err
475: 			}
476: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "pause"); err != nil {
477: 				return err
478: 			}
479: 			// Commit any dirty changes in the worktree before pausing.
480: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" {
481: 				commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] update from '%s' on %s (paused)", rec.Title, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
482: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "add", "-A").Run()
483: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath, "commit", "-m", commitMsg, "--allow-empty").Run()
484: 			}
485: 			// Kill the tmux session using the kas_-prefixed name.
486: 			_ = exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title)).Run()
487: 			// Remove the worktree (preserve the branch).
488: 			if rec.Worktree.WorktreePath != "" && rec.Worktree.RepoPath != "" {
489: 				if err := exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "remove", "--force", rec.Worktree.WorktreePath).Run(); err != nil {
490: 					return fmt.Errorf("remove worktree: %w", err)
491: 				}
492: 				_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "prune").Run()
493: 			}
494: 			// Update state: mark as paused and clear the worktree path.
495: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
496: 				r.Status = instancePaused
497: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
498: 				return nil
499: 			}); err != nil {
500: 				return err
501: 			}
502: 			fmt.Printf("paused: %s\n", rec.Title)
503: 			return nil
504: 		},
505: 	}
506: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(pauseCmd)
507: 
508: 	// kas instance resume <title>
509: 	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
510: 		Use:   "resume <title>",
511: 		Short: "resume a paused agent instance",
512: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
513: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
514: 			title := args[0]
515: 			state := config.LoadState()
516: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
517: 			if err != nil {
518: 				return err
519: 			}
520: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
521: 			if err != nil {
522: 				return err
523: 			}
524: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "resume"); err != nil {
525: 				return err
526: 			}
527: 			if rec.Worktree.RepoPath == "" || rec.Worktree.BranchName == "" {
528: 				return fmt.Errorf("instance %q has no stored worktree metadata; cannot resume", rec.Title)
529: 			}
530: 			// Derive the worktree path from the repo path and session name.
531: 			worktreePath := rec.Worktree.WorktreePath
532: 			if worktreePath == "" {
533: 				// Reconstruct using the conventional path: <worktree-root>/<title>
534: 				worktreePath = rec.Path
535: 			}
536: 			// Re-add the git worktree on the preserved branch.
537: 			if err := exec.Command("git", "-C", rec.Worktree.RepoPath, "worktree", "add", worktreePath, rec.Worktree.BranchName).Run(); err != nil {
538: 				return fmt.Errorf("recreate worktree: %w", err)
539: 			}
540: 			// Reconstruct the full program command (env vars + flags) matching the original session.
541: 			program := buildResumeCommand(rec, worktreePath)
542: 			// Start a new tmux session using the kas_-prefixed name.
543: 			sessionName := kasTmuxName(rec.Title)
544: 			if err := exec.Command("tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", sessionName, "-c", worktreePath, program).Run(); err != nil {
545: 				return fmt.Errorf("start tmux session: %w", err)
546: 			}
547: 			// Update state: mark as running and store the restored worktree path.
548: 			if err := updateInstanceInState(state, rec.Title, func(r *instanceRecord) error {
549: 				r.Status = instanceRunning
550: 				r.Worktree.WorktreePath = worktreePath
551: 				return nil
552: 			}); err != nil {
553: 				return err
554: 			}
555: 			fmt.Printf("resumed: %s\n", rec.Title)
556: 			return nil
557: 		},
558: 	}
559: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(resumeCmd)
560: 
561: 	// kas instance send <title> <prompt>
562: 	sendCmd := &cobra.Command{
563: 		Use:   "send <title> <prompt>",
564: 		Short: "send a prompt to an agent instance",
565: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(2),
566: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
567: 			title := args[0]
568: 			prompt := args[1]
569: 			state := config.LoadState()
570: 			records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
571: 			if err != nil {
572: 				return err
573: 			}
574: 			rec, err := findInstanceData(records, title)
575: 			if err != nil {
576: 				return err
577: 			}
578: 			if err := validateStatusForAction(rec, "send"); err != nil {
579: 				return err
580: 			}
581: 			// Use kas_-prefixed session name to match how the session was created.
582: 			if err := exec.Command("tmux", "send-keys", "-t", kasTmuxName(rec.Title), prompt, "Enter").Run(); err != nil {
583: 				return fmt.Errorf("send keys: %w", err)
584: 			}
585: 			fmt.Printf("sent to %s\n", rec.Title)
586: 			return nil
587: 		},
588: 	}
589: 	instanceCmd.AddCommand(sendCmd)
590: 
591: 	return instanceCmd
592: }

163: // fullInstanceRecord returns an instanceRecord with every field set to a
164: // non-zero/non-default value.  Used by round-trip tests to verify that
165: // removeInstanceFromState and updateInstanceInState do not silently drop any
166: // field when they deserialise and re-serialise the state JSON.
167: func fullInstanceRecord(title string) instanceRecord {
168: 	return instanceRecord{
169: 		Title:                  title,
170: 		Path:                   "/worktrees/" + title,
171: 		Branch:                 "feat/" + title,
172: 		Status:                 instanceRunning,
173: 		Height:                 48,
174: 		Width:                  220,
175: 		CreatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
176: 		UpdatedAt:              time.Date(2026, 1, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
177: 		Program:                "claude",
178: 		AutoYes:                true,
179: 		SkipPermissions:        true,
180: 		TaskFile:               title + ".md",
181: 		AgentType:              "coder",
182: 		TaskNumber:             3,
183: 		WaveNumber:             2,
184: 		PeerCount:              4,
185: 		IsReviewer:             false,
186: 		ImplementationComplete: true,
187: 		SoloAgent:              false,
188: 		QueuedPrompt:           "hello from " + title,
189: 		ReviewCycle:            2,
190: 		Worktree: instanceWorktree{
191: 			RepoPath:      "/repo",
192: 			WorktreePath:  "/repo/.worktrees/" + title,
193: 			SessionName:   title,
194: 			BranchName:    "feat/" + title,
195: 			BaseCommitSHA: "deadbeef" + title,
196: 		},
197: 		DiffStats: instanceDiffStats{
198: 			Added:   10,
199: 			Removed: 5,
200: 			Content: "some diff for " + title,
201: 		},
202: 	}
203: }

214: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Width, got.Width, "Width")

216: 	assert.True(t, want.UpdatedAt.Equal(got.UpdatedAt), "UpdatedAt: want %v got %v", want.UpdatedAt, got.UpdatedAt)
217: 	assert.Equal(t, want.Program, got.Program, "Program")
218: 	assert.Equal(t, want.AutoYes, got.AutoYes, "AutoYes")
219: 	assert.Equal(t, want.SkipPermissions, got.SkipPermissions, "SkipPermissions")
220: 	assert.Equal(t, want.TaskFile, got.TaskFile, "TaskFile")
221: 	assert.Equal(t, want.AgentType, got.AgentType, "AgentType")
222: 	assert.Equal(t, want.TaskNumber, got.TaskNumber, "TaskNumber")
223: 	assert.Equal(t, want.WaveNumber, got.WaveNumber, "WaveNumber")

226: 	assert.Equal(t, want.ImplementationComplete, got.ImplementationComplete, "ImplementationComplete")

229: 	assert.Equal(t, want.ReviewCycle, got.ReviewCycle, "ReviewCycle")

240: // TestRemoveInstanceFromState_PreservesFields verifies that removing one instance
241: // does not corrupt any fields of the remaining instances during the JSON round-trip.
242: // This is the regression test for the data-loss bug where instanceRecord was
243: // incomplete and silently dropped ~15 fields from every surviving instance.
244: func TestRemoveInstanceFromState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {
245: 	keeper := fullInstanceRecord("keeper")
246: 	goner := fullInstanceRecord("goner")
247: 
248: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{keeper, goner})
249: 
250: 	err := removeInstanceFromState(state, "goner")
251: 	require.NoError(t, err)
252: 
253: 	remaining, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
254: 	require.NoError(t, err)
255: 	require.Len(t, remaining, 1, "expected exactly one instance to remain after removal")
256: 
257: 	assertRecordFieldsEqual(t, keeper, remaining[0])
258: }

261: // removes only the named instance and leaves all others intact (titles only).
262: func TestRemoveInstanceFromState_RemovesOnlyTarget(t *testing.T) {
263: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{
264: 		{Title: "alpha", Status: instanceRunning, Program: "claude"},
265: 		{Title: "beta", Status: instanceRunning, Program: "claude"},
266: 		{Title: "gamma", Status: instanceRunning, Program: "claude"},
267: 	})
268: 
269: 	err := removeInstanceFromState(state, "beta")
270: 	require.NoError(t, err)

273: 	require.NoError(t, err)
274: 	require.Len(t, remaining, 2)
275: 	assert.Equal(t, "alpha", remaining[0].Title)
276: 	assert.Equal(t, "gamma", remaining[1].Title)

279: // TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields verifies that updating one field of one
280: // instance does not corrupt any fields of the other instances during the JSON
281: // round-trip.  This is the regression test for the data-loss bug in pause/resume.
282: func TestUpdateInstanceInState_PreservesFields(t *testing.T) {
283: 	untouched := fullInstanceRecord("untouched")
284: 	target := fullInstanceRecord("target")
285: 	target.Status = instancePaused // start paused so we can "resume" it
286: 
287: 	state := newTestStateFromRecords(t, []instanceRecord{untouched, target})
288: 
289: 	// Simulate what pause does: sets status to paused and clears the worktree path.
290: 	err := updateInstanceInState(state, "target", func(r *instanceRecord) error {
291: 		r.Status = instancePaused
292: 		r.Worktree.WorktreePath = ""
293: 		return nil
294: 	})
295: 	require.NoError(t, err)
296: 
297: 	records, err := loadInstanceRecords(state)
298: 	require.NoError(t, err)
299: 	require.Len(t, records, 2)
300: 
301: 	// untouched instance must have ALL its fields preserved.
302: 	var gotUntouched, gotTarget instanceRecord
303: 	for _, r := range records {
304: 		switch r.Title {
305: 		case "untouched":
306: 			gotUntouched = r
307: 		case "target":
308: 			gotTarget = r
309: 		}
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	assertRecordFieldsEqual(t, untouched, gotUntouched)
313: 
314: 	// target must have only Status and Worktree.WorktreePath changed.
315: 	assert.Equal(t, instancePaused, gotTarget.Status, "target.Status should be paused")
316: 	assert.Empty(t, gotTarget.Worktree.WorktreePath, "target.Worktree.WorktreePath should be cleared")
317: 	// All other fields of target should be preserved.
318: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Height, gotTarget.Height, "target.Height")
319: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Width, gotTarget.Width, "target.Width")
320: 	assert.Equal(t, target.AutoYes, gotTarget.AutoYes, "target.AutoYes")
321: 	assert.Equal(t, target.SkipPermissions, gotTarget.SkipPermissions, "target.SkipPermissions")
322: 	assert.Equal(t, target.TaskNumber, gotTarget.TaskNumber, "target.TaskNumber")
323: 	assert.Equal(t, target.WaveNumber, gotTarget.WaveNumber, "target.WaveNumber")
324: 	assert.Equal(t, target.PeerCount, gotTarget.PeerCount, "target.PeerCount")
325: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ImplementationComplete, gotTarget.ImplementationComplete, "target.ImplementationComplete")
326: 	assert.Equal(t, target.QueuedPrompt, gotTarget.QueuedPrompt, "target.QueuedPrompt")
327: 	assert.Equal(t, target.ReviewCycle, gotTarget.ReviewCycle, "target.ReviewCycle")
328: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.RepoPath, gotTarget.Worktree.RepoPath, "target.Worktree.RepoPath")
329: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.SessionName, gotTarget.Worktree.SessionName, "target.Worktree.SessionName")
330: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BranchName, gotTarget.Worktree.BranchName, "target.Worktree.BranchName")
331: 	assert.Equal(t, target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, gotTarget.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA, "target.Worktree.BaseCommitSHA")
332: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Added, gotTarget.DiffStats.Added, "target.DiffStats.Added")
333: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Removed, gotTarget.DiffStats.Removed, "target.DiffStats.Removed")
334: 	assert.Equal(t, target.DiffStats.Content, gotTarget.DiffStats.Content, "target.DiffStats.Content")
335: }

19: // setupTestPlanState creates an in-memory SQLite store pre-populated with two
20: // test plans and returns the store, the repo root, and the project name.
21: func setupTestPlanState(t *testing.T) (taskstore.Store, string, string) {
22: 	t.Helper()
23: 	store := taskstore.NewTestSQLiteStore(t)
24: 
25: 	root := t.TempDir()

27: 
28: 	require.NoError(t, store.Create(project, taskstore.TaskEntry{
29: 		Filename:    "test-plan.md",
30: 		Status:      taskstore.StatusReady,
31: 		Description: "test plan",
32: 		Branch:      "plan/test-plan",
33: 		CreatedAt:   time.Now(),
34: 	}))
35: 	require.NoError(t, store.Create(project, taskstore.TaskEntry{
36: 		Filename:    "implementing-plan.md",
37: 		Status:      taskstore.Status("implementing"),
38: 		Description: "implementing plan",
39: 		Branch:      "plan/implementing-plan",
40: 		CreatedAt:   time.Now(),
41: 	}))
42: 
43: 	return store, root, project
44: }

299: func TestPlanCLI_FromWorktreeContext(t *testing.T) {
300: 	// Create main repo structure
301: 	mainRepo := t.TempDir()
302: 	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(mainRepo, ".git", "worktrees", "wt-plans"), 0o755))
303: 
304: 	// Create a worktree that has a .git file pointing to the main repo's worktrees dir
305: 	worktree := t.TempDir()
306: 	gitFile := fmt.Sprintf("gitdir: %s/.git/worktrees/wt-plans\n", mainRepo)
307: 	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(worktree, ".git"), []byte(gitFile), 0o644))
308: 	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
309: 		filepath.Join(mainRepo, ".git", "worktrees", "wt-plans", "commondir"),
310: 		[]byte("../..\n"), 0o644,
311: 	))
312: 
313: 	// Test resolveRepoInfo end-to-end by chdir'ing into the worktree.
314: 	origDir, err := os.Getwd()
315: 	require.NoError(t, err)
316: 	t.Cleanup(func() { require.NoError(t, os.Chdir(origDir)) })
317: 	require.NoError(t, os.Chdir(worktree))
318: 
319: 	repoRoot, project, err := resolveRepoInfo()
320: 	require.NoError(t, err)
321: 	assert.Equal(t, mainRepo, repoRoot)
322: 	assert.Equal(t, filepath.Base(mainRepo), project)
323: 
324: 	// Populate the store and verify plan operations succeed
325: 	store := taskstore.NewTestSQLiteStore(t)
326: 	require.NoError(t, store.Create(project, taskstore.TaskEntry{
327: 		Filename:    "worktree-plan.md",
328: 		Status:      taskstore.StatusReady,
329: 		Description: "worktree plan",
330: 		Branch:      "plan/worktree-plan",
331: 		CreatedAt:   time.Now(),
332: 	}))
333: 
334: 	// executeTaskList should succeed with project name
335: 	output := executeTaskList(project, "", store)
336: 	assert.Contains(t, output, "worktree-plan.md")
337: 
338: 	// executeTaskSetStatus should succeed
339: 	err = executeTaskSetStatus(project, "worktree-plan.md", "done", true, store)
340: 	require.NoError(t, err)
341: 
342: 	// executeTaskTransition: reset then transition
343: 	err = executeTaskSetStatus(project, "worktree-plan.md", "ready", true, store)
344: 	require.NoError(t, err)
345: 	newStatus, err := executeTaskTransition(project, "worktree-plan.md", "plan_start", store)
346: 	require.NoError(t, err)
347: 	assert.Equal(t, "planning", newStatus)
348: }

604: func TestExecuteTaskMerge_IntegrationWithRealRepo(t *testing.T) {
605: 	store := taskstore.NewTestSQLiteStore(t)
606: 	project := "merge-integration"
607: 	branch := "plan/int-merge"
608: 
609: 	// Create a real git repo.
610: 	repo := t.TempDir()
611: 	runGit := func(args ...string) {
612: 		t.Helper()
613: 		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", repo}, args...)...)
614: 		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
615: 		require.NoErrorf(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
616: 	}
617: 	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
618: 	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.com")
619: 	runGit("config", "user.name", "test")
620: 	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(repo, "README.md"), []byte("init\n"), 0644))
621: 	runGit("add", ".")
622: 	runGit("commit", "-m", "initial")
623: 
624: 	// Create the plan branch with a commit.
625: 	runGit("branch", branch)
626: 	runGit("checkout", branch)
627: 	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(repo, "feature.go"), []byte("package feature\n"), 0644))
628: 	runGit("add", ".")
629: 	runGit("commit", "-m", "add feature")
630: 	runGit("checkout", "main")
631: 
632: 	require.NoError(t, store.Create(project, taskstore.TaskEntry{
633: 		Filename: "int-merge.md",
634: 		Status:   taskstore.Status("reviewing"),
635: 		Branch:   branch,
636: 	}))
637: 
638: 	err := executeTaskMerge(repo, project, "int-merge.md", store)
639: 	require.NoError(t, err)
640: 
641: 	// Verify FSM transitioned to done.
642: 	ps, _ := taskstate.Load(store, project, "")
643: 	entry, _ := ps.Entry("int-merge.md")
644: 	assert.Equal(t, taskstate.StatusDone, entry.Status)
645: 
646: 	// Verify branch was deleted.
647: 	out, _ := exec.Command("git", "-C", repo, "branch", "--list", branch).CombinedOutput()
648: 	assert.Empty(t, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)))
649: }

657: 	runGit := func(args ...string) {
658: 		t.Helper()
659: 		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", repo}, args...)...)
660: 		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
661: 		require.NoErrorf(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
662: 	}
663: 	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
664: 	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.com")
665: 	runGit("config", "user.name", "test")
666: 	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(repo, "README.md"), []byte("init\n"), 0644))
667: 	runGit("add", ".")
668: 	runGit("commit", "-m", "initial")
669: 	runGit("branch", branch)
670: 
671: 	require.NoError(t, store.Create(project, taskstore.TaskEntry{
672: 		Filename: "int-redo.md",
673: 		Status:   taskstore.Status("done"),
674: 		Branch:   branch,
675: 	}))
676: 
677: 	err := executeTaskStartOver(repo, project, "int-redo.md", store)
678: 	require.NoError(t, err)
679: 
680: 	// Verify FSM transitioned to planning.
681: 	ps, _ := taskstate.Load(store, project, "")
682: 	entry, _ := ps.Entry("int-redo.md")
683: 	assert.Equal(t, taskstate.StatusPlanning, entry.Status)
684: 
685: 	// Verify branch still exists (recreated from HEAD).
686: 	out, _ := exec.Command("git", "-C", repo, "branch", "--list", branch).CombinedOutput()
687: 	assert.Contains(t, string(out), "int-redo")
688: }
689: 
690: // TestPlanList_WithStore verifies that executeTaskListWithStore works with a
691: // store-backed HTTP server, returning plan entries from the remote store.
692: func TestPlanList_WithStore(t *testing.T) {
693: 	backend := taskstore.NewTestSQLiteStore(t)
694: 	srv := httptest.NewServer(taskstore.NewHandler(backend))
695: 	defer srv.Close()
696: 
697: 	err := backend.Create("test-project", taskstore.TaskEntry{
698: 		Filename: "test.md", Status: "ready", Description: "test plan",
699: 	})
700: 	require.NoError(t, err)
701: 
702: 	output := executeTaskListWithStore(srv.URL, "test-project")
703: 	assert.Contains(t, output, "test.md")
704: 	assert.Contains(t, output, "ready")
705: }

<content>1: package auditlog
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"database/sql"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"strings"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // register sqlite driver
10: )
11: 
12: const auditSchema = `
13: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_events (
14: 	id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
15: 	kind           TEXT    NOT NULL,
16: 	timestamp      TEXT    NOT NULL,
17: 	project        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
18: 	plan_file      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
19: 	instance_title TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
20: 	agent_type     TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
21: 	wave_number    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
22: 	task_number    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
23: 	message        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
24: 	detail         TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
25: 	level          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'
26: );
27: 
28: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_project_ts ON audit_events(project, timestamp DESC);
29: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_plan ON audit_events(plan_file, timestamp DESC);
30: `
31: 
32: const maxQueryLimit = 500
33: 
34: // SQLiteLogger is a Logger backed by a SQLite database.
35: type SQLiteLogger struct {
36: 	db *sql.DB
37: }
38: 
39: // NewSQLiteLogger opens (or creates) a SQLite database at dbPath, runs the
40: // audit_events schema, and returns a ready-to-use logger.
41: // Use ":memory:" for an in-memory database (useful in tests).
42: func NewSQLiteLogger(dbPath string) (*SQLiteLogger, error) {
43: 	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath)
44: 	if err != nil {
45: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite db for audit log: %w", err)
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	if _, err := db.Exec(auditSchema); err != nil {
49: 		db.Close()
50: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("run audit log schema: %w", err)
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return &SQLiteLogger{db: db}, nil
54: }
55: 
56: // Emit inserts an audit event into the database. If the event's Timestamp is
57: // zero, it is set to time.Now(). Emit is synchronous and safe to call from the
58: // bubbletea Update goroutine.
59: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Emit(e Event) {
60: 	if e.Timestamp.IsZero() {
61: 		e.Timestamp = time.Now()
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	const q = `
65: 		INSERT INTO audit_events
66: 			(kind, timestamp, project, plan_file, instance_title, agent_type,
67: 			 wave_number, task_number, message, detail, level)
68: 		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
69: 	`
70: 	level := e.Level
71: 	if level == "" {
72: 		level = "info"
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	_, _ = l.db.Exec(q,
76: 		string(e.Kind),
77: 		auditFormatTime(e.Timestamp),
78: 		e.Project,
79: 		e.TaskFile,
80: 		e.InstanceTitle,
81: 		e.AgentType,
82: 		e.WaveNumber,
83: 		e.TaskNumber,
84: 		e.Message,
85: 		e.Detail,
86: 		level,
87: 	)
88: }
89: 
90: // Query returns events matching the filter, ordered newest-first.
91: // Limit is capped at 500.
92: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Query(f QueryFilter) ([]Event, error) {
93: 	limit := f.Limit
94: 	if limit <= 0 || limit > maxQueryLimit {
95: 		limit = maxQueryLimit
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	var conditions []string
99: 	var args []any
100: 
101: 	if f.Project != "" {
102: 		conditions = append(conditions, "project = ?")
103: 		args = append(args, f.Project)
104: 	}
105: 	if f.TaskFile != "" {
106: 		conditions = append(conditions, "plan_file = ?")
107: 		args = append(args, f.TaskFile)
108: 	}
109: 	if f.InstanceTitle != "" {
110: 		conditions = append(conditions, "instance_title = ?")
111: 		args = append(args, f.InstanceTitle)
112: 	}
113: 	if len(f.Kinds) > 0 {
114: 		placeholders := make([]string, len(f.Kinds))
115: 		for i, k := range f.Kinds {
116: 			placeholders[i] = "?"
117: 			args = append(args, string(k))
118: 		}
119: 		conditions = append(conditions, "kind IN ("+strings.Join(placeholders, ", ")+")")
120: 	}
121: 	if !f.After.IsZero() {
122: 		conditions = append(conditions, "timestamp > ?")
123: 		args = append(args, auditFormatTime(f.After))
124: 	}
125: 	if !f.Before.IsZero() {
126: 		conditions = append(conditions, "timestamp < ?")
127: 		args = append(args, auditFormatTime(f.Before))
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	q := `
131: 		SELECT id, kind, timestamp, project, plan_file, instance_title,
132: 		       agent_type, wave_number, task_number, message, detail, level
133: 		FROM audit_events
134: 	`
135: 	if len(conditions) > 0 {
136: 		q += " WHERE " + strings.Join(conditions, " AND ")
137: 	}
138: 	q += fmt.Sprintf(" ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT %d", limit)
139: 
140: 	rows, err := l.db.Query(q, args...)
141: 	if err != nil {
142: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("query audit events: %w", err)
143: 	}
144: 	defer rows.Close()
145: 
146: 	var events []Event
147: 	for rows.Next() {
148: 		var e Event
149: 		var ts string
150: 		if err := rows.Scan(
151: 			&e.ID,
152: 			(*string)(&e.Kind),
153: 			&ts,
154: 			&e.Project,
155: 			&e.TaskFile,
156: 			&e.InstanceTitle,
157: 			&e.AgentType,
158: 			&e.WaveNumber,
159: 			&e.TaskNumber,
160: 			&e.Message,
161: 			&e.Detail,
162: 			&e.Level,
163: 		); err != nil {
164: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan audit event: %w", err)
165: 		}
166: 		e.Timestamp = auditParseTime(ts)
167: 		events = append(events, e)
168: 	}
169: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
170: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("iterate audit events: %w", err)
171: 	}
172: 	return events, nil
173: }
174: 
175: // Close releases the database connection.
176: func (l *SQLiteLogger) Close() error {
177: 	return l.db.Close()
178: }
179: 
180: // auditFormatTime formats a time.Time as RFC3339Nano for storage.
181: // Zero time returns empty string.
182: func auditFormatTime(t time.Time) string {
183: 	if t.IsZero() {
184: 		return ""
185: 	}
186: 	return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
187: }
188: 
189: // auditParseTime parses an RFC3339Nano string.
190: // Returns zero time on empty or invalid input.
191: func auditParseTime(s string) time.Time {
192: 	if s == "" {
193: 		return time.Time{}
194: 	}
195: 	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)
196: 	if err != nil {
197: 		return time.Time{}
198: 	}
199: 	return t
200: }

(End of file - total 200 lines)
</content>

1766: // retryFailedWaveTasks retries all failed tasks in the current wave by re-spawning them.
1767: // Old failed instances are removed first to prevent ghost duplicates that accumulate
1768: // across retries and all get marked ImplementationComplete when waves finish.
1769: func (m *home) retryFailedWaveTasks(orch *orchestration.WaveOrchestrator, entry taskstate.TaskEntry) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1770: 	tasks := orch.RetryFailedTasks()
1771: 	if len(tasks) == 0 {
1772: 		return m, nil
1773: 	}
1774: 
1775: 	// Build a set of task numbers being retried for fast lookup.
1776: 	retryingTasks := make(map[int]bool, len(tasks))
1777: 	for _, t := range tasks {
1778: 		retryingTasks[t.Number] = true
1779: 	}
1780: 
1781: 	// Remove old failed instances for the tasks being retried.
1782: 	// Collect first to avoid mutating the list while iterating.
1783: 	planFile := orch.TaskFile()
1784: 	var staleInsts []*session.Instance
1785: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1786: 		if inst.TaskFile == planFile && retryingTasks[inst.TaskNumber] {
1787: 			staleInsts = append(staleInsts, inst)
1788: 		}
1789: 	}
1790: 	for _, inst := range staleInsts {
1791: 		m.nav.RemoveByTitle(inst.Title)
1792: 		m.removeFromAllInstances(inst.Title)
1793: 	}
1794: 
1795: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("retrying %d failed task(s) in wave %d",
1796: 		len(tasks), orch.CurrentWaveNumber()))
1797: 	return m.spawnWaveTasks(orch, tasks, entry)
1798: }

1800: // discoverTmuxSessions returns a tea.Cmd that lists all kas_ tmux sessions (managed + orphaned).
1801: func (m *home) discoverTmuxSessions() tea.Cmd {
1802: 	knownNames := make([]string, 0, len(m.allInstances))
1803: 	for _, inst := range m.allInstances {
1804: 		if inst.Started() && inst.TmuxAlive() {
1805: 			knownNames = append(knownNames, tmux.ToKasTmuxNamePublic(inst.Title))
1806: 		}
1807: 	}
1808: 	return func() tea.Msg {
1809: 		sessions, err := tmux.DiscoverAll(cmd2.MakeExecutor(), knownNames)
1810: 		return tmuxSessionsMsg{sessions: sessions, err: err}
1811: 	}
1812: }

1814: // buildChatAboutTaskPrompt builds the custodian prompt for a chat-about-plan session.
1815: func buildChatAboutTaskPrompt(planFile string, entry taskstate.TaskEntry, question string) string {
1816: 	name := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
1817: 	var sb strings.Builder
1818: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("You are answering a question about the plan '%s'.\n\n", name))
1819: 	sb.WriteString("## Plan Context\n\n")
1820: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Plan:** %s\n", planFile))
1821: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Status:** %s\n", entry.Status))
1822: 	if entry.Description != "" {
1823: 		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Description:** %s\n", entry.Description))
1824: 	}
1825: 	if entry.Branch != "" {
1826: 		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Branch:** %s\n", entry.Branch))
1827: 	}
1828: 	if entry.Topic != "" {
1829: 		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Topic:** %s\n", entry.Topic))
1830: 	}
1831: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\nRetrieve the full plan with `kas task show %s` for details.\n\n", planFile))
1832: 	sb.WriteString("## User Question\n\n")
1833: 	sb.WriteString(question)
1834: 	return sb.String()
1835: }
1836: 
1837: // spawnChatAboutTask spawns a custodian agent pre-loaded with the plan context and user question.
1838: func (m *home) spawnChatAboutTask(planFile, question string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1839: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1840: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("no task state loaded"))
1841: 	}
1842: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
1843: 	if !ok {
1844: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", planFile))
1845: 	}
1846: 	prompt := buildChatAboutTaskPrompt(planFile, entry, question)
1847: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
1848: 	title := planName + "-chat"
1849: 
1850: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1851: 		Title:     title,
1852: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
1853: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1854: 		TaskFile:  planFile,
1855: 		AgentType: session.AgentTypeFixer,
1856: 	})
1857: 	if err != nil {
1858: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1859: 	}
1860: 	inst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
1861: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1862: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 5
1863: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "preparing chat..."
1864: 
1865: 	// Use the plan's branch worktree if available, otherwise main.
1866: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1867: 	branch := m.taskBranch(planFile)
1868: 	if branch != "" {
1869: 		shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
1870: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1871: 			if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1872: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1873: 			}
1874: 			if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
1875: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1876: 			}
1877: 			err := inst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
1878: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1879: 		}
1880: 	} else {
1881: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1882: 			if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, m.activeRepoPath); err != nil {
1883: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1884: 			}
1885: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1886: 		}
1887: 	}
1888: 
1889: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned custodian chat for %s", taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1890: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1891: 		auditlog.WithInstance(title),
1892: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1893: 	)
1894: 
1895: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1896: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1897: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.RequestWindowSize, startCmd)
1898: }

1901: func (m *home) adoptOrphanSession(item overlay.TmuxBrowserItem) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1902: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{

62: 	dir, err := GetConfigDir()

65: 		return DefaultState()

28: // GetConfigDir returns the XDG-compliant config directory (~/.config/kasmos/).
29: // On first call without that directory, it attempts to migrate legacy directories
30: // (.klique, then .hivemind) via rename. If migration fails it returns the old path.
31: func GetConfigDir() (string, error) {
32: 	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
33: 	if err != nil {
34: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get config home directory: %w", err)
35: 	}
36: 
37: 	target := filepath.Join(home, ".config", "kasmos")
38: 
39: 	// Fast path: target already exists.
40: 	if _, statErr := os.Stat(target); statErr == nil {
41: 		return target, nil
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	// Try each legacy directory in preference order.
45: 	for _, legacy := range []string{
46: 		filepath.Join(home, ".klique"),
47: 		filepath.Join(home, ".hivemind"),
48: 	} {
49: 		if _, statErr := os.Stat(legacy); statErr != nil {
50: 			continue
51: 		}
52: 		// Ensure ~/.config/ parent exists before rename.
53: 		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0755); mkErr != nil {
54: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to create %s: %v", filepath.Dir(target), mkErr)
55: 			return legacy, nil
56: 		}
57: 		if mvErr := os.Rename(legacy, target); mvErr != nil {
58: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to migrate %s to %s: %v", legacy, target, mvErr)
59: 			return legacy, nil
60: 		}
61: 		return target, nil
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	// No legacy dir found — return target path; caller creates it on first write.
65: 	return target, nil
66: }

155: // findCommand locates name via the user's login shell and PATH.
156: // It sources the appropriate rc file so aliases are visible, then falls
157: // back to exec.LookPath when the shell subprocess fails.
158: func findCommand(name string) (string, error) {
159: 	shell := os.Getenv("SHELL")
160: 	if shell == "" {
161: 		shell = "/bin/bash"
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	var inline string
165: 	switch {
166: 	case strings.Contains(shell, "zsh"):
167: 		inline = fmt.Sprintf("source ~/.zshrc &>/dev/null || true; which %s", name)
168: 	case strings.Contains(shell, "bash"):
169: 		inline = fmt.Sprintf("source ~/.bashrc &>/dev/null || true; which %s", name)
170: 	default:
171: 		inline = fmt.Sprintf("which %s", name)
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	out, err := exec.Command(shell, "-c", inline).Output()
175: 	if err == nil && len(out) > 0 {
176: 		if p := parseCommandOutput(string(out)); p != "" {
177: 			return p, nil
178: 		}
179: 	}

181: 	// Fallback: consult PATH directly.

184: 	}

967: // handleTmuxBrowserAction dispatches actions from the tmux session browser overlay.
968: // browser is the TmuxBrowserOverlay captured BEFORE HandleKey was called (so SelectedItem is valid).
969: // action is the Result.Action string returned by HandleKey.
970: func (m *home) handleTmuxBrowserAction(browser *overlay.TmuxBrowserOverlay, action string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
971: 	switch action {
972: 	case "": // dismissed without action
973: 		m.overlays.Dismiss()
974: 		m.state = stateDefault
975: 		return m, nil
976:

980: 		}
981: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
982: 		if item.Name == "" {
983: 			return m, nil
984: 		}
985: 		name := item.Name
986: 		browser.RemoveSelected()
987: 		if browser.IsEmpty() {
988: 			m.overlays.Dismiss()

996:

999: 			return m, nil
1000: 		}
1001: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
1002: 		if item.Name == "" {
1003: 			return m, nil
1004: 		}
1005: 		m.state = stateDefault
1006: 		return m.adoptOrphanSession(overlay.TmuxBrowserItem{
1007: 			Name:  item.Name,
1008: 			Title: item.Title,

1010:

1013: 			return m, nil
1014: 		}
1015: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
1016: 		if item.Name == "" {
1017: 			return m, nil
1018: 		}
1019: 		m.state = stateDefault
1020: 		name := item.Name
1021: 		return m, tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("tmux", "attach-session", "-t", name), func(err error) tea.Msg {
1022: 			return tmuxAttachReturnMsg{}
1023: 		})
1024: 
1025: 	default:

1027: 	}

1030: // isLocked returns true if the given stage cannot be triggered given the current plan status.
1031: // The context menu already gates which forward stages are offered, so this only
1032: // guards against truly nonsensical transitions (e.g. marking "finished" when already done).
1033: func isLocked(status taskstate.Status, stage string) bool {
1034: 	switch stage {
1035: 	case "plan", "implement", "implement_direct", "solo", "review":
1036: 		// Forward progression is always allowed — the FSM helpers
1037: 		// (fsmSetImplementing, fsmSetReviewing) walk through intermediate states.
1038: 		return false
1039: 	case "finished":
1040: 		return status != taskstate.StatusReviewing
1041: 	default:
1042: 		return true
1043: 	}

8: // NewStoreFromConfig creates a Store from a plan store URL and project name.
9: // If storeURL is empty, it returns (nil, nil) — the caller should fall
10: // back to legacy plan-state.json behavior.
11: // The returned store uses lazy connection: the URL is validated syntactically
12: // but no network connection is made until the first operation (or Ping).
13: func NewStoreFromConfig(storeURL, project string) (Store, error) {
14: 	if storeURL == "" {
15: 		return nil, nil // no remote store configured
16: 	}
17: 	return NewHTTPStore(storeURL, project), nil
18: }
19: 
20: // ResolvedDBPath returns the filesystem path that the factory would use for a
21: // local SQLite taskstore. It reads the XDG-compliant config directory
22: // (~/.config/kasmos/) and appends "taskstore.db". This path is shared with
23: // the auditlog SQLiteLogger so both can coexist in the same database file
24: // (each using a separate table).
25: func ResolvedDBPath() string {
26: 	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
27: 	if err != nil {
28: 		return filepath.Join(".", "taskstore.db")
29: 	}
30: 	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config", "kasmos", "taskstore.db")

16: // NewServeCmd returns the `kas serve` cobra command.
17: // It starts an HTTP server backed by a SQLite task store.
18: func NewServeCmd() *cobra.Command {
19: 	var (
20: 		port int
21: 		db   string
22: 		bind string
23: 	)
24: 
25: 	cmd := &cobra.Command{
26: 		Use:   "serve",
27: 		Short: "start the task store HTTP server",
28: 		Long:  "Start an HTTP server that exposes task state over a REST API backed by SQLite.",
29: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
30: 			store, err := taskstore.NewSQLiteStore(db)
31: 			if err != nil {
32: 				return fmt.Errorf("open task store: %w", err)
33: 			}
34: 			defer store.Close()
35: 
36: 			handler := taskstore.NewHandler(store)
37: 			addr := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", bind, port)
38: 
39: 			srv := &http.Server{
40: 				Addr:    addr,
41: 				Handler: handler,
42: 			}
43: 
44: 			fmt.Printf("task store listening on http://%s (db: %s)\n", addr, db)
45: 
46: 			// Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
47: 			ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
48: 			defer stop()
49: 
50: 			errCh := make(chan error, 1)
51: 			go func() {
52: 				if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
53: 					errCh <- err
54: 				}
55: 				close(errCh)
56: 			}()
57: 
58: 			select {
59: 			case err := <-errCh:
60: 				return err
61: 			case <-ctx.Done():
62: 				fmt.Println("\nshutting down...")
63: 				shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
64: 				defer cancel()
65: 				return srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
66: 			}
67: 		},
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	defaultDB := taskstore.ResolvedDBPath()
71: 
72: 	cmd.Flags().IntVar(&port, "port", 7433, "port to listen on")
73: 	cmd.Flags().StringVar(&db, "db", defaultDB, "path to the SQLite database file")
74: 	cmd.Flags().StringVar(&bind, "bind", "0.0.0.0", "address to bind to")

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # CLI Monitoring & Observability Commands
2: 
3: **Goal:** Add CLI commands for querying the audit log, managing orphaned tmux sessions, and viewing instance status summaries. Closes Category 5 (Monitoring & Observability) in the UI/CLI feature parity report.
4: 
5: **Order:** 3 of 4 in CLI parity series. Independent of `cli-signal-processing.md` — CAN run in parallel with it (and with `cli-clickup-integration.md`). Only requires `cli-plan-lifecycle.md` to be done (for shared CLI infrastructure patterns).
6: 
7: **Depends on:** `cli-plan-lifecycle.md`
8: 
9: **Architecture:** Three independent subcommand groups:
10: - `kas audit` — queries the existing SQLite audit log (`config/auditlog/`). Data already exists, just needs a CLI reader.
11: - `kas tmux` — wraps tmux CLI to discover orphaned `kas_*` sessions, adopt them into the instance list, or kill them. Reuses logic from `app_actions.go:handleTmuxBrowserAction`.
12: - `kas instance status` — adds a summary subcommand showing running/ready/paused/killed counts.
13: 
14: Each is self-contained and testable independently.
15: 
16: **Tech Stack:** Go, cobra, `config/auditlog`, `session/tmux`, `config.State`
17: 
18: **Size:** Small-Medium (estimated ~3 hours, 3 tasks, 1 wave — all tasks are independent)
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## Wave 1
23: 
24: ### Task 1 — `kas audit` subcommand
25: 
26: Add `kas audit list` cobra command that queries the existing SQLite audit log.
27: 
28: **Files:**
29: - `cmd/audit.go` — new cobra command: `kas audit list [--limit=N] [--event=X]`
30: - `cmd/audit_test.go` — table-driven tests for flag parsing, output format, empty log
31: 
32: **Details:**
33: - Implement these top-level functions in `cmd/audit.go`:
34: 
35: ```go
36: func NewAuditCmd() *cobra.Command
37: func executeAuditList(logger auditlog.Logger, project string, limit int, event string) (string, error)
38: func openAuditLogger() (*auditlog.SQLiteLogger, error)
39: ```
40: 
41: - Follow the existing cmd-layer pattern from `cmd/instance.go` and `cmd/task.go`: keep cobra `RunE` thin, put deterministic logic in `execute...` helpers, and wrap operational errors with `%w` context (`fmt.Errorf("query audit events: %w", err)`).
42: - Use `resolveRepoInfo()` (already in `cmd/task.go`) to derive `project`; query only that project via `auditlog.QueryFilter{Project: project, Limit: limit}` so output mirrors the active repo context used by the TUI.
43: - Build event filtering by setting `Kinds` only when `--event` is non-empty:
44: 
45: ```go
46: filter := auditlog.QueryFilter{Project: project, Limit: limit}
47: if event != "" {
48: 	filter.Kinds = []auditlog.EventKind{auditlog.EventKind(event)}
49: }
50: ```
51: 
52: - Use `taskstore.ResolvedDBPath()` in `openAuditLogger()` (same shared DB path used in `app/app.go` when initializing the audit logger), then `auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(dbPath)`.
53: - Keep `--limit` default at 50; reject non-positive values in command argument handling (return a validation error before querying).
54: - Format output with `text/tabwriter` using columns: `TIME`, `EVENT`, `DETAILS`. Derive `DETAILS` as `Message` plus `Detail` when present (for richer context). Timestamp format should be stable and sortable (e.g. `2006-01-02 15:04:05`).
55: - Empty-state behavior must exactly print `no audit entries found` (lowercase, no punctuation), then return nil error.
56: - Required imports for `cmd/audit.go`:
57: 
58: ```go
59: import (
60: 	"fmt"
61: 	"strings"
62: 	"text/tabwriter"
63: 
64: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/auditlog"
65: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskstore"
66: 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
67: )
68: ```
69: 
70: - Testing guidance for `cmd/audit_test.go`: use in-memory logger (`auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(":memory:")`), seed mixed events, and table-drive cases for default limit, `--event` filter, table output shape, and empty log output.
71: 
72: - Command wiring should mirror the existing thin `RunE` style in `cmd/task.go:547` and `cmd/instance.go:396`:
73: 
74: ```go
75: func NewAuditCmd() *cobra.Command {
76: 	auditCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "audit", Short: "query audit events"}
77: 	var limit int
78: 	var event string
79: 	listCmd := &cobra.Command{
80: 		Use:  "list",
81: 		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
82: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
83: 			if limit <= 0 {
84: 				return fmt.Errorf("limit must be > 0")
85: 			}
86: 			_, project, err := resolveRepoInfo()
87: 			if err != nil {
88: 				return err
89: 			}
90: 			logger, err := openAuditLogger()
91: 			if err != nil {
92: 				return err
93: 			}
94: 			defer logger.Close()
95: 			out, err := executeAuditList(logger, project, limit, event)
96: 			if err != nil {
97: 				return err
98: 			}
99: 			fmt.Print(out)
100: 			return nil
101: 		},
102: 	}
103: 	listCmd.Flags().IntVar(&limit, "limit", 50, "max rows")
104: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&event, "event", "", "event kind filter")
105: 	auditCmd.AddCommand(listCmd)
106: 	return auditCmd
107: }
108: ```
109: 
110: - `executeAuditList` should be deterministic and pure (string builder + filter construction only):
111:   - Build filter from arguments first, then call `logger.Query(filter)`.
112:   - For each event row, format `TIME` with local display format `2006-01-02 15:04:05`.
113:   - Build `DETAILS` as:
114:     - `Message` when `Detail == ""`
115:     - `Message + " | " + Detail` when both are non-empty
116:     - `Detail` when `Message == ""`
117:   - Return wrapped query errors exactly as `fmt.Errorf("query audit events: %w", err)` (pattern from `config/auditlog/sqlite.go:142`).
118: 
119: - Edge cases to handle explicitly:
120:   - `limit <= 0` should fail fast in `RunE` before opening DB (no side effects).
121:   - `logger.Query` returning empty slice should produce exactly `no audit entries found\n`.
122:   - Unknown `--event` values are not validated in cmd layer; they map to `auditlog.EventKind(event)` and naturally return zero rows.
123:   - `openAuditLogger()` should only wrap initialization failures (`open sqlite db for audit log`, `run audit log schema`) and not swallow them.
124: 
125: - Test function layout (table-driven, testify) in `cmd/audit_test.go`:
126:   - `TestAuditList_FormatsTable`
127:   - `TestAuditList_Empty`
128:   - `TestAuditList_EventFilter`
129:   - `TestAuditCmd_RejectsNonPositiveLimit`
130:   - Use `auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(":memory:")`, seed with at least two different kinds, and assert header row includes `TIME\tEVENT\tDETAILS`.
131: 
132: 
133: - Codebase anchors to mirror while implementing:
134:   - `cmd/task.go:543` (`show`) and `cmd/task.go:563` (`update-content`) demonstrate the preferred cmd pattern here: resolve repo context, call a pure helper, print returned string, and keep side effects localized.
135:   - `app/app.go:388` and `app/app.go:435` confirm audit logging uses the same DB file as taskstore via `taskstore.ResolvedDBPath()`; `openAuditLogger()` should follow that exact path source.
136:   - `config/auditlog/sqlite.go:92` already guarantees `ORDER BY timestamp DESC`; keep cmd output in query order (no extra sorting).
137: - Root wiring requirement so the command is reachable: extend `NewRootCmd()` in `cmd/cmd.go` to register `NewAuditCmd()` alongside existing subcommands.
138: - Keep `executeAuditList` deterministic by isolating formatting helpers inside `cmd/audit.go` (no stdout writes in helpers):
139: 
140: ```go
141: func formatAuditDetails(message, detail string) string
142: func renderAuditRows(events []auditlog.Event) string
143: ```
144: 
145: - Testing specifics from current patterns:
146:   - Follow `cmd/instance_test.go` style (`assert` + `require`, table-driven cases, helper constructors).
147:   - In seeded events, set explicit `Timestamp` values so assertions on table row ordering are deterministic even when rows are emitted rapidly.
148:   - Add one invalid-limit command execution test that asserts the exact error text `limit must be > 0` before any DB open attempt.
149: - Keep `openAuditLogger()` intentionally thin so upstream error text from `auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger` remains intact for assertions:
150: 
151: ```go
152: func openAuditLogger() (*auditlog.SQLiteLogger, error) {
153: 	return auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(taskstore.ResolvedDBPath())
154: }
155: ```
156: 
157: - Add command reachability coverage (same style as `cmd/serve_test.go`) to avoid wiring regressions:
158:   - `cmd, _, err := NewRootCmd().Find([]string{"audit", "list"})`
159:   - assert `err == nil` and `cmd.Name() == "list"`.
160: 
161: - Additional implementation guardrails from codebase reads:
162:   - Keep cobra/output boundaries identical to `cmd/task.go:543` and `cmd/task.go:552`: `RunE` does repo resolution + dependency setup, while `executeAuditList` returns `(string, error)` with zero stdout writes.
163:   - Render timestamps with `event.Timestamp.Local().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")` so CLI output aligns with existing UI display behavior in `app/app_state.go:1967`; storage remains UTC via `auditFormatTime` in `config/auditlog/sqlite.go:186`.
164:   - Preserve query ordering as returned (already DESC in `config/auditlog/sqlite.go:138`), and avoid re-sorting in cmd helpers.
165:   - Add tiny pure-helper tests to lock behavior without cobra plumbing:
166: 
167: ```go
168: func TestFormatAuditDetails(t *testing.T)
169: func TestRenderAuditRows_HeaderAndOrder(t *testing.T)
170: ```
171: 
172: **Tests:**
173: - `go test ./cmd/... -run TestAudit -count=1`
174: 
175: ---
176: 
177: ### Task 2 — `kas tmux` subcommand group
178: 
179: Add `kas tmux list`, `kas tmux adopt`, and `kas tmux kill` cobra commands for orphan tmux session management.
180: 
181: **Files:**
182: - `cmd/tmux.go` — new cobra command group with `list`, `adopt <session> <title>`, `kill <session>`
183: - `cmd/tmux_test.go` — unit tests (mock tmux output where needed)
184: 
185: **Details:**
186: - Important constraint: do **not** import `session/tmux` from `cmd` package (import cycle: `session/tmux -> cmd`). Follow the same local-mirror strategy documented in `cmd/instance.go` for tmux name/state handling.
187: - Implement local parsing around `tmux ls -F` using the same format string as `session/tmux/tmux_session.go`:
188: 
189: ```go
190: "#{session_name}|#{session_created}|#{session_windows}|#{session_attached}|#{window_width}|#{window_height}"
191: ```
192: 
193: - Implement these signatures in `cmd/tmux.go`:
194: 
195: ```go
196: type tmuxSessionRow struct {
197: 	Name     string
198: 	Title    string
199: 	Created  time.Time
200: 	Windows  int
201: 	Attached bool
202: 	Width    int
203: 	Height   int
204: 	Managed  bool
205: }
206: 
207: func NewTmuxCmd() *cobra.Command
208: func discoverKasSessions(exec Executor, known map[string]struct{}) ([]tmuxSessionRow, error)
209: func executeTmuxList(state config.StateManager, exec Executor) (string, error)
210: func executeTmuxAdopt(state config.StateManager, sessionName, title, repoRoot string, now time.Time, exec Executor) error
211: func executeTmuxKill(state config.StateManager, sessionName string, exec Executor) error
212: ```
213: 
214: - Build `known` tmux names from persisted instances via existing helpers in the same package: `loadInstanceRecords(state)` + `kasTmuxName(record.Title)` (pattern from `app/app_state.go:discoverTmuxSessions`).
215: - `kas tmux list`: discover all `kas_` sessions, then print only orphan rows (`Managed == false`) with `NAME`, `TITLE`, `WINDOWS`, `ATTACHED`, `AGE`; if none, print `no orphan tmux sessions found`.
216: - `kas tmux adopt <session-name> <title>`:
217:   - Validate session exists in current orphan set (exact name match; no prefix matching).
218:   - Validate `title` is non-empty and does not collide with an existing instance title.
219:   - Append a new `instanceRecord` to state without mutating existing records (preserve full JSON round-trip behavior established in `cmd/instance.go`).
220:   - Seed adopted record as `Status: instanceReady`, `Program: "unknown"`, `Path: repoRoot`, `CreatedAt/UpdatedAt: now`.
221: - `kas tmux kill <session-name>`:
222:   - Validate target exists in orphan set before killing.
223:   - Execute `tmux kill-session -t <session-name>` through injected `Executor`.
224:   - Return wrapped execution errors (`fmt.Errorf("kill tmux session %s: %w", sessionName, err)`).
225: - Treat `tmux ls` exit errors indicating no server/no sessions as an empty list (same behavior as `session/tmux.DiscoverAll`); malformed output lines should be skipped, not fatal.
226: - Required imports for `cmd/tmux.go`:
227: 
228: ```go

230: 	"encoding/json"
231: 	"fmt"
232: 	"os/exec"

237: 
238: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config"
239: 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
240: )
241: ```
242: 
243: - Testing guidance for `cmd/tmux_test.go`: table-drive parser/list formatting, orphan filtering, adopt validation failures, and state preservation checks (reuse `newTestStateFromRecords` + `assertRecordFieldsEqual` patterns from `cmd/instance_test.go`).
244:

246: 
247: ```go
248: func NewTmuxCmd() *cobra.Command {
249: 	tmuxCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "tmux", Short: "manage orphan tmux sessions"}
250: 	listCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "list", Args: cobra.NoArgs, RunE: ...}
251: 	adoptCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "adopt <session> <title>", Args: cobra.ExactArgs(2), RunE: ...}
252: 	killCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "kill <session>", Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1), RunE: ...}
253: 	tmuxCmd.AddCommand(listCmd, adoptCmd, killCmd)
254: 	return tmuxCmd
255: }
256: ```
257: 
258: - `discoverKasSessions` implementation shape:
259:   - Execute `tmux ls -F "#{session_name}|#{session_created}|#{session_windows}|#{session_attached}|#{window_width}|#{window_height}"` through injected `Executor.Output`.
260:   - Treat `*exec.ExitError` as empty list (same contract as `session/tmux.DiscoverAll` in `session/tmux/tmux_session.go:595`).

metadata.preview:
# CLI Monitoring & Observability Commands

**Goal:** Add CLI commands for querying the audit log, managing orphaned tmux sessions, and viewing instance status summaries. Closes Category 5 (Monitoring & Observability) in the UI/CLI feature parity report.

**Order:** 3 of 4 in CLI parity series. Independent of `cli-signal-processing.md` — CAN run in parallel with it (and with `cli-clickup-integration.md`). Only requires `cli-plan-lifecycle.md` to be done (for shared CLI infrastructure patterns).

**Depends on:** `cli-plan-lifecycle.md`

**Architecture:** Three independent subcommand groups:
- `kas audit` — queries the existing SQLite audit log (`config/auditlog/`). Data already exists, just needs a CLI reader.
- `kas tmux` — wraps tmux CLI to discover orphaned `kas_*` sessions, adopt them into the instance list, or kill them. Reuses logic from `app_actions.go:handleTmuxBrowserAction`.
- `kas instance status` — adds a summary subcommand showing running/ready/paused/killed counts.

Each is self-contained and testable independently.

**Tech Stack:** Go, cobra, `config/auditlog`, `session/tmux`, `config.State`

**Size:** Small-Medium (estimated ~3 hours, 3 tasks, 1 wave — all tasks are independent)

---
